name: Coated Olives with Spicy Yogurt
intro: oil, a sprinkle of the sumac and cilantro, and the last of the lemon juice.

ingredients.item: green chile, with seeds, coarsely chopped
cups/20 g cilantro leaves, coarsely chopped
cup/20 g flat-leaf parsley leaves, coarsely chopped
medium preserved lemon (1½ oz/45 g), skin and flesh coarsely chopped
cup/80 g panko bread crumbs, processed to a very fine crumb
eggs, beaten rounded 1 cup/160 g pitted green olives sunflower oil, for frying

method: Place the chile, spices, sugar, cilantro, parsley, preserved lemon, and olive oil in a small blender and blitz to a rough paste. Pour into a small bowl and set aside until required.
Place the bread crumbs, flour, and eggs in 3 separate small bowls.
Drain the olives from their preserving juices and pat dry with paper towels.
Once dry, take 1 olive and dip in the flour, then the egg, then finally coat well in the panko. Place on a plate to one side. Repeat with the rest of the olives.

Pour the sunflower oil to a depth of 1¼-inches/3-cm into a small saucepan and place over medium-high heat. Once the oil is hot, add a couple of the olives. They should take 1 minute to go golden brown and need to be turned over during the process. If they brown too quickly, turn the heat down.
Remove the olives from the oil and transfer to paper towels. Repeat with the rest of the olives until they are all cooked.
Spoon the yogurt into a shallow bowl, then swirl the green paste into it (don’t mix them completely). Pile the olives, warm or at room temperature, into the center and serve.
